entry.title: Re: Planned values not appearing for activity in CJ20N
entry.content: Hi Customer, I've removed the "SAP Project and Resource Management" tag and replaced it with the "PLM Project System (PS)" tag as this query is more relevant to SAP S/4HANA Project System. This will help with engaging with the correct experts to assist with your query Best Regards, Tim

entry.title: Re: Agent builder in Joule Studio is now generally available: Build your own Joule agents today!
entry.content: This is a very informative and timely article, thanks for this. I have one question around the Joule Studio availability in US West region. Currently it is only available for US East (AWS, Azure), and US Central (GCP). My customer is looking for Azure in US West. I assume enabling it in a separate US East sub account and then deploying it is always an option.

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud: Best Practices for Folder Organization and Governance
entry.content:
Introduction

In SAP Analytics Cloud projects, a significant amount of effort is spent on building models, stories, and planning applications. However, one critical topic is often underestimated: how these objects are organized. In many customer environments, reports and models are scattered across different folders without a clear structure, making it difficult to control access, ensure consistency, and maintain content over time. This is not just a usability issue, it is a governance challenge. A well-designed folder structure is a foundational element for scalable, secure, and maintainable SAC landscapes.

 

Why Folder Organization Matters in SAC?

Folder structure is not a cosmetic choice, it is a governance decision.

Number of objects like Stories, Models, Themes, Insights are rapidly increasing in SAC environments. It is crucial to keep these objects in organized way. Otherwise, this can cause important security and governance issues. Without well-planned folder structure,  important objects like corporate stories, models etc. can be located in My Files or Public Folders with ambiguous ownerships. It is not certain that who is using these files and folders and who are the owners. Without discrimination of folders, contents that should be tested first can be accidently used with Production purposes. Also with authorization complexity, unnecessary accesses can cause security issues. As a result, weak folder structure leads low maintainability, low security and high operational risk.

 

Core Folder Types

Understanding the core folder types in SAP Analytics Cloud is essential for building a sustainable governance model. Each folder type serves a specific purpose, and misusing them often leads to security risks, ownership issues, and long-term maintenance problems. The two primary folder types My Files and Public should be clearly differentiated and used intentionally.

My Files – Personal Workspace

My Files is designed as a personal working area for individual users in SAP Analytics Cloud. Content stored in My Files is accessible only by the owner and is not governed by role-based access control, which makes it suitable for early-stage development, drafts, experiments, and personal analysis. It serves as a sandbox environment where users can prototype ideas or perform temporary testing without impacting shared content. However, because objects in My Files are not centrally visible or governed unless explicitly shared, they are not appropriate for long-term or productive use.

As a best practice, any object that is actively used by business users should not located in My Files.

Public – Enterprise Content Area

Public is the central, governed content area in SAP Analytics Cloud and serves as the foundation for enterprise analytics. Unlike personal folders, content stored in Public is managed through role-based access control, enabling controlled visibility, clear ownership, and secure collaboration across teams and departments. Public folders support structured hierarchies, which makes them ideal for implementing environment separation such as DEV, TEST, and PROD, as well as department-based organization. All shared stories, models, planning applications, and related objects intended for long-term use should reside in the Public folder structure. By enforcing consistent naming conventions and access rules within Public, organizations can significantly improve maintainability, reduce operational risk, and establish a scalable governance model for SAP Analytics Cloud.

As a best practice, all productive SAC content should be located in the Public folder hierarchy.

 

DEV – TEST – PROD Folder Strategy

A clear DEV–TEST–PROD folder strategy is one of the most critical elements of governance in SAP Analytics Cloud. Separation  between development - testing and production provides a strong folder discipline. Especially, the separation is crucial if you are in a single tenant landscape. Even in multi-tenant landscapes (Dev-Test-Prod), this is also recommended since the transportation between tenants is not mandatory and system-driven. 

A structured folder strategy introduces a clear content lifecycle, reduces operational risk, and creates transparency for both IT and business users.

Recommended High-Level Folder Structure

/Public
├─ _DEV 
├─ _TEST
└─ _PROD

Environment Responsibilities

DEV

  • Active development and frequent changes
  • Broad access for developers and consultants
  • Experimental and incomplete content allowed

TEST

  • Business validation and user acceptance testing
  • Limited write access
  • Focus on functional correctness and data validation

PROD

  • Approved, production-ready content only
  • Strict access control
  • Stable objects consumed by business users

Object Type-Based Folder Design

Standardized subfolder design based on object types is a key enabler for consistency and scalability in SAP Analytics Cloud. By structuring folders according to object types—such as Stories, Models, Analytic Applications, Data Actions, and Allocations—organizations create a predictable and intuitive navigation experience across DEV, TEST, and PROD environments.

Department-Based Folder Design

By grouping stories, models, and applications under department-specific folders such as Finance, Sales, Supply Chain, or HR, ownership and accountability become immediately clear. This approach simplifies access management through role-based authorization, reduces the risk of unintended data exposure, and improves discoverability for business users. When combined with a DEV–TEST–PROD structure, department-based folders ensure that each team works within a clearly defined and governed space, supporting scalability and long-term maintainability of the SAC environment.

 

Workspaces

Workspaces in SAP Analytics Cloud are designed to support collaboration by grouping content, users, and access rights within a shared space. They can be useful for project-based or temporary initiatives where multiple users work closely on the same set of stories or models.

However, from a governance perspective, I personally avoid using Workspaces as a primary content container. When a Workspace is deleted, all content stored within it is permanently removed, which introduces a significant operational risk. For this reason, Workspaces should be used selectively and primarily for short-lived collaboration, while long-term and productive content should always reside in the Public folder structure.

You can navigate to this blog post by @ninak1 to learn workspaces.

 

Authorization Best Practices (Team-Based Governance)

Effective authorization in SAP Analytics Cloud should be managed through teams, not individual users. Assigning access directly to users quickly becomes unmanageable as environments grow, leading to inconsistent permissions, security risks, and high maintenance effort. A team-based authorization model provides scalability, transparency, and long-term governance by clearly separating responsibilities and standardizing access patterns.

Recommended Team Personas

A clear persona model is essential for aligning technical permissions with business responsibilities. The following team structure covers most SAC scenarios while remaining simple and scalable:

Admin

  • System and tenant administration
  • User, team, and role management
  • Full access across DEV, TEST, and PROD

Developer

  • Model, story, and application development
  • Full write access in DEV
  • Limited or no write access in PROD

Editor / Analyst 

  • Ability to create own stories and perform small adjustments
  • Typically business power users or advanced analysts
  • Write access to specific folders, without system-level permissions

Why “Editor / Analyst”?
Compared to “Developer,” this role better reflects business-driven content creation and controlled flexibility without exposing technical or administrative capabilities.

Viewer

  • Read-only access to validated, productive content
  • No content creation or modification rights

Folder Authorization via Teams

Folders should always be shared with teams, not individual users. This approach ensures that access rights are role-based, auditable, and easy to maintain.

Best Practices

  • DEV folders → Developer team (edit), Admin team (full)
  • TEST folders → Developer / Editor (limited edit), Viewer (read)
  • PROD folders → Viewer (read), Editor (controlled edit), Admin (full)

By assigning folder permissions at the team level, access changes are handled simply by adding or removing users from teams—without touching folder settings.

Role Assignment Strategy

In addition to folder access, SAC roles define what users can do at the platform level. These roles should also be assigned to teams rather than individuals.

Example Role Mapping

  • Admin team → BI Admin role
  • Developer team → Content Creator role
  • Editor / Analyst team → Content Creator (restricted by folder access)
  • Viewer team → Viewer role

Best Practice:
Assign roles and folder permissions to teams—not to individual users.

Here is the link of the detailed blog post for Security Concept by @Matthew_Shaw

 

What to Do Next

To apply these best practices in your SAP Analytics Cloud environment, start with the following steps:

  • Define your governance model first
    Agree on how content should be developed, validated, and consumed before creating folders.

  • Separate DEV, TEST, and PROD clearly
    Create dedicated environment folders under Public and enforce a forward-only content flow.

  • Organize content by department and object type
    Use department-based folders combined with standardized subfolders (Stories, Models, Data Actions, etc.).

  • Establish a clear naming convention
    Document and communicate a simple, consistent naming standard for all SAC objects.

  • Adopt team-based authorization
    Create teams based on personas (Admin, Developer, Editor/Analyst, Viewer) and assign both roles and folder permissions to teams—not individual users.

  • Review and clean up existing content
    Identify unused, duplicated, or misplaced objects and move productive content into the governed Public structure.

 

Sources

  • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/sap-analytics-cloud-content-organization-and-authorization/ba-p/13350602
  • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/sap-analytics-cloud-security-concepts-and-best-practice/ba-p/13416999
  • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/sap-analytics-cloud-workspaces/ba-p/13531009

entry.title: How to Enable LMS in SAP SuccessFactors for the first time
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I’m looking for guidance on enabling SAP SuccessFactors Learning (LMS) for the first time in a tenant.

I would appreciate help on:

Prerequisites that need to be completed before enabling LMS

Any configurations or switches required at the instance level

Integration or provisioning steps (if any)

Common pitfalls to avoid during the initial setup

If there’s an official SAP guide, KBA, or best-practice documentation, please feel free to share that as well.

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: Authorization activities before and after SAP S/4HANA...
entry.content:
Hi, 

There are standard procedures to follow that are well documented in sap. The most critical tasks involve

1. Checking simplification impacts,

2. Adjusting roles to new authorization objects.

3. Re‑running SU25 after the upgrade to regenerate proposals.

See the link below and remember to choose the correct version of you upgrade. --> ( Hence in the link below - go to dropdown of 1809 and choose 2023.)

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/a525c5402c594b8b91b8c54caa54f904/840b9e577ce96c10e10000000a441470.html?locale=en-US&version=1809.latest

entry.title: i need access to some content in the digital library
entry.content:
I need access to this in the digital library but am getting an error that i dont have permission.. this link was in the SAP documentation

https://help.sap.com/docs/link-disclaimer?site=https%3A%2F%2Fd.dam.sap.com%2Fs%2Fc%

The SAP doc link is: https://help.sap.com/docs/customer-data-platform/onboarding/emarsys-integration-with-customer-data-platform

entry.title: SAP DMC: How to use WIP Batch
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

I have successfully completed the execution of a discrete production order in SAP Digital Manufacturing in Dev. (Big Shoutout to @ManoelCosta  for assisting my queries regarding DMC)

Going forward, my organization operates in the packaging industry and currently manages Work in Process (WIP) using WIP batches in SAP S/4HANA. I would like guidance on how WIP can be handled in SAP Digital Manufacturing.

Specifically, could you please advise on best practices or recommended guidelines for managing WIP in SAP DM? At present, our organization uses movement types 521 and 522 to maintain WIP traceability and to move material to and from WIP storage locations in S/4HANA.

Any guidance, documentation, or practical insights on aligning this WIP process with SAP Digital Manufacturing would be greatly appreciated.

Best regards,
Aziz Khan

SAP Digital Manufacturing SAP Digital Manufacturing Insights

entry.title: Re: Request for CDS View with Line Item Data of Recurring...
entry.content:
Dear @S0027269767,

In Public Cloud recurring journal entries are handled as a template object that later generates normal posted journal entries when you run/trigger posting. SAP delivers I_RecurringJournalEntry for the template header but what’s currently documented/released for Public Cloud there is no released CDS interface view that exposes the template’s line items

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/ca03bbfc892d4474be69934d6f5c783d.html

What to use instead:

If your reporting requirement is line items of the postings created from recurring runs then you should report on the posted journal entry line items, using the released Journal Entry item views:

  • I_JournalEntryItem released interface view for journal entry item details (Universal Journal / ACDOCA-based)

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c0c54048d35849128be8e872df5bea6d/47dce95760521070e10000000a44147b.html

  • I_JournalEntryItemCube released cube for journal entry item analytics in Public Cloud packages

More details below:

https://api.sap.com/cdsviews/I_JOURNALENTRYITEMCUBE

  • C_JournalEntryItemBrowser released consumption view used by the Display Line Items in General Ledger app, good confirmation it’s the standard line-item dataset in Public Cloud

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/15ee31fac4be4e6497a76abe449fe49c.html

How to tie it back to recurring

1. Use Manage Recurring Journal Entries to trigger due postings and review posting status/results this is where you identify which accounting documents were generated from which recurring template

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/114f17567f98692de10000000a4450e5.html

2. Then read the line items for those generated accounting documents from I_JournalEntryItem / I_JournalEntryItemCube / C_JournalEntryItemBrowser using the standard keys, typically Company Code + Fiscal Year + Accounting Document + Item

The generation step is also reflected in the standard job Post Recurring Journal Entries, which posts the due recurring originals into normal accounting documents

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/cd189f57e7356d10e10000000a441470.html

Summary:

If you need template line items before posting in Public Cloud there isn’t a released CDS view that I can point you to for recurring-template items. Practically that means:

  • adjust the requirement to report on posted results recommended / stable via the views above

Best Regards,

Dawid

Please note our S4 is on rise CDC. Can you please let us know if anything else is required?

Thanks,

Prasanthi

entry.title: How to extend the program SAPF124 of F.13 to retrieve its results
entry.content:
• Introduction
  • Selection screen parameters and result example
  • SAPF124 logic
  • Subroutine ECHTLAUF with I_KOART being 'D', 'K' or 'S'
  • Subroutine SPERREN_KONTO (lock)
  • Subroutine AUSGLEICH with I_SUM = sum of amounts of all documents with the same clearing criteria
  • Subroutine ACC_APPEND_DATA
  • Enhancements
  • Basic code to run SAPF124
  • ABAP type for the collected data

Introduction

F.13 is the transaction to clear the open items automatically. For functional information, see Automatic Clearing | SAP Help Portal.

The result is a spool which contains a log with all results.

To analyze the results by program, it's easier to create a few enhancements to F.13 (program SAPF124) to retrieve the log as several internal tables, rather than decoding the spool (called "ABAP List" hereafter), because the latter is often subject to changes after an upgrade.

Note that some people recommend in the forum to use POSTING_INTERFACE_CLEARING but I couldn't find any official information saying that it's allowed for automating F.13.

Before creating the enhancements to the program SAPF124, it's important to understand the logic of the program. Also, the names of some of the selection screen parameters and an example of the output ABAP List are useful.

Warning: in this blog post, I don't post the code I used, but I explain which subroutines can be enhanced.

 

Selection screen parameters and result example

Example of the output ABAP List. Here F.13 ran in test mode so there's no clearing document created (so, the number remains blank). We can see the list of cleared documents at the top, then the additional log and the error log at the bottom.

 

SAPF124 logic

AT SELECTION-SCREEN:

  • Call the subroutine CHECK_ECHTL
    • In productive mode (X_TESTL not checked), it displays the warning FG013 “This program run is a production run” as a popup.
    • It also calculates X_ECHTL = “X” or “ ”.
      • “X” means that the execution is in productive mode (X_TESTL not checked).

 

START-OF-SELECTION:

  • Call the subroutine KONTOTABELLEN_FUELLEN
    • Fills the 3 lists of objects to process: customers (internal table TDEBI), suppliers (internal table TKREDI) and accounts (internal table TSAKO), according to the selection screen parameters, based on the tables BSIK, BSIS and SKB1 respectively.
  • Logic for the 3 lists
    • Call the subroutine DEBI_VERARBEITEN if the "Customers" box is checked
      • LOOP AT TDEBI (customers)
        • Call subroutine ECHTLAUF with KOART = 'D'
    • Call the subroutine KREDI_VERARBEITEN if the "Suppliers" box is checked
      • LOOP AT TKREDI (suppliers)
        • Call subroutine ECHTLAUF with KOART = 'K'
    • Call the subroutine SAKO_VERARBEITEN if the "Accounts" box is checked
      • LOOP AT TSAKO (accounts)
        • Call subroutine ECHTLAUF with KOART = 'S'

 

Event END-OF-SELECTION:

  • Call the subroutine ACC_AUSGABE_ADDITION_LOG
    • Write the internal table IPROT (additional log) to the ABAP List.
  • IF X_FEHLER = 'X':
    • Call the subroutine ACC_AUSGABE_FAULT_LOG
      • It writes the internal table FEHLPROT (error messages) to the ABAP List.

 

Subroutine ECHTLAUF with I_KOART being 'D', 'K' or 'S'

  • For information, depending on I_KOART being 'D', 'K' or 'S', the data will be taken respectively from TDEBI, TKREDI or TSAKO.
  • Call subroutine SPERREN_KONTO to lock the object to process
  • Call subroutine INDEXTABELLEN_FUELLEN
    • READ_BSID or READ_BSIK or READ_BSIS to build the groups of clearing criteria XBSIDGR or XBSIKGR or XBSISGR, depending on I_KOART = 'D', 'K' or 'S' respectively.
      • APPEND XBSID or XBSIK or XBSIS
      • COLLECT XBSIDGR or XBSIKGR or XBSISGR depending on I_KOART = 'D', 'K' or 'S' respectively:
        • Each line is a group of clearing criteria for which FI documents may be cleared altogether.
        • The field XBSIDGR-BET_BW or XBSIKGR-BET_BW or XBSISGR-BET_TW contains the sum of FI documents which can be zero (they can be cleared) or another number (they cannot be cleared).
    • LOOP AT XBSIDGR or XBSIKGR or XBSISGR depending on I_KOART = 'D', 'K' or 'S' respectively
      • Call subroutine AUSGLEICH USING I_SUM = XBSIDGR-BET_BW or XBSIKGR-BET_BW or XBSISGR-BET_TW

 

Subroutine SPERREN_KONTO (lock)

  • If KOART = 'D', call ENQUEUE_EFKNB1A with tdebi-bukrs, tdebi-kunnr.
  • If KOART = 'K', call ENQUEUE_EFLFB1A with tkredi-bukrs, tkredi-lifnr.
  • If KOART = 'S', call ENQUEUE_EFSKB1A with tsako-bukrs, tsako-hkont, tsako-hbkid, tsako-hktid.

 

Subroutine AUSGLEICH with I_SUM = sum of amounts of all documents with the same clearing criteria

It checks if the clearing of one group of documents with the same clearing criteria is possible. If yes, it’s calling a given transaction code (whose code may vary) via Batch Input to create one clearing document.

The subroutine has the following logic:

  • Call the subroutine BELEGE_AUSGEBEN (collect the list of documents to be cleared)
    • Loop at all the lines of XBSID, XBSIK or XBSIS corresponding to the current grouping criteria XBSIDGR, XBSIKGR or XBSISGR depending on KOART = 'D', 'K' or 'S' respectively.
      • Call the subroutine RECORD_DOCUMENT
        • APPEND GS_DLIST TO GT_DLIST. It's the list of FI documents corresponding to the current clearing criteria group.
  • If I_SUM = 0 (the documents of the current group have a total amount of 0, so they can be cleared).
    • Call the subroutine AUGDT_PRUEFEN
      • Call the subroutine CALL_TRANSACTION if it's a productive run
        • Batch input CALL TRANSACTION FB1D, FB1K or FB1S/FB1SL depending on KOART = 'D', 'K' or 'S' respectively.
        • The batch input messages are stored into the internal table MESSTAB
        • Call the subroutine MESSTAB_AUSWERTEN
          • If MESSTAB contains the success message F5312 "Document & was posted in company code &", AUGBLNR (global variable) = MSGV1 (document number)
          • Call the subroutine RECORD_CLEARING_INFO with I_CHAR = 'AUGBL'
            • Call subroutine ACC_APPEND_DATA with parameters AUGBLNR, clearing date and ID_MESSAGE = ‘AUGBL’ to display the documents to be cleared and the clearing document created.
            • IMPORTANT: GT_DLIST is cleared (the list of documents to be cleared). 
              • Call subroutine RECORD_CLEARING_INFO with I_CHAR = code expressing errors (‘NOCLEAR’, ‘NOTZERO’, etc.)
          • If X_FEHLER = 'X'.
            • Call subroutine MESSTAB_LESEN which feeds the internal table FEHLPROT (displayed error log) from MESSTAB (error messages from the batch input)
  • Else if clearing is permitted with tolerance
    • TODO
  • Else (if no clearing)
    • Call subroutine ACC_APPEND_DATA with parameter ID_MESSAGE = ‘NOTZERO’.
    • If there’s some clearing tolerance, add the error to the internal table IPROT (additional log).

 

Subroutine ACC_APPEND_DATA

It receives the parameters:

  • ID_AUGBLNR = clearing document created
  • ID_AUGDT = clearing date
  • ID_MESSAGE = ‘AUGBL’ (clearing) or others (no clearing), etc.
    • AUGBL: The clearing has been done, i.e. the clearing document has been created, the global variable AUGBL contains this clearing document, the log contains F5312 (*).
    • NOTZERO: the clearing is not possible because the sum of amounts of the documents is not zero.
    • AUGDT: Test mode successful (in test mode, the F5312 and F5263 (*) messages are absent and there is no error).
    • NOCLEAR: The reconciliation document has failed (the F5312 (*) message is absent and no tolerance is requested or a tolerance is requested but F5263 is absent).
    • NOCLEARTOL: Failed (the F5312 (*) message is absent and a tolerance is requested and F5263 is present).
    • ENQ: failed because the object of the current clearing criteria could not be locked
    • Other values: Failed
    • (*) Batch Input may return one of the following messages:
      • F5312: "Document & was posted in company code &"
      • F5263: "The difference is too large for clearing"

This subroutine has the following logic:

  • Call the subroutine WRITE_LISTE if the output of cleared/clearing documents has been requested in the selection screen. This subroutine does:
    • LOOP AT GT_DLIST INTO GS_DLIST (list of documents to be cleared)
      • The document is written to the ABAP List.
    • Call subroutine CHECK_SUMME
      • The clearing criteria are written to the ABAP List on one line, including the new clearing document if one has been created.
  • GT_DLIST is emptied (the list of documents to be cleared).

 

Enhancements

The parameter X_FEHLER (to display error messages in the ABAP List) is absolutely necessary to run several SAPF124 subroutines which contain information about the cleared documents.

The following subroutines may be enhanced to obtain information about the cleared documents used and the clearing documents created:

  • Begin of CHECK_ECHTL
    • In real mode, it displays the warning FG013 “This program run is a production run”.
    • It also calculates X_ECHTL = “X” or “ ”. “X” means that the execution is in real mode (case F.13 is not called in test mode i.e., checkbox X_TESTL = space/false).
    • This message is annoying if SAPF124 is called by a custom program in a loop.
    • Do this enhancement:
      • Keep calculating X_ECHTL = “X” or “ ” but don’t display the warning by using ABAP “RETURN” to not execute the standard code of the subroutine.
  • Begin of ACC_APPEND_DATA
    • It’s called after the logic to create one clearing document, whatever it has succeeded (parameter ID_MESSAGE = ‘AUGBL’) or failed (other values).
    • Do this enhancement:
      • Collect the list of documents cleared (internal table GT_DLIST), the clearing status (ID_MESSAGE) and the clearing document created (ID_AUGBLNR). The clearing document will be empty if the creation failed.
  • Begin of MESSTAB_LESEN
    • It’s called when the creation of the cleared document failed via Batch Input and it feeds the internal table FEHLPROT from MESSTAB (error messages from Batch Input).
    • Do this enhancement:
      • Collect the list of documents cleared (internal table GT_DLIST) and the error messages from the internal table FEHLPROT.
  • Begin of ACC_AUSGABE_FAULT_LOG
    • It’s called at the end, but only if the selection screen option X_FEHLER was selected.
    • Do this enhancement:
      • Export the data previously collected by the enhancements to the ABAP memory (EXPORT TO MEMORY ID).

 

Basic code to run SAPF124

Transaction F.13 starts SAPF124 with the SAP&F124 variant, so I'm doing the same thing. I haven't checked to see if there's a difference without the variant.

The actual content is obtained by reading the data collected by the enhancements in the ABAP memory (IMPORT FROM MEMORY ID).

SUBMIT sapf124
    USING SELECTION-SET 'SAP&F124'
    WITH bukrx   IN ir_bukrs     " Company
    WITH gjahx   IN ir_gjahr     " Year
    WITH docnr   IN ir_belnr     " Document Number
    WITH x_kunnr  = iv_xkunnr    " Check "Customers"
    WITH kontd   IN ir_kunnr     " Customers
    WITH x_lifnr  = iv_xlifnr    " Check "Suppliers"
    WITH kontk   IN ir_lifnr     " Suppliers
    WITH x_saknr  = iv_xsaknr    " Check "Accounts"
    WITH konts   IN ir_saknr     " Accounts
    WITH x_testl  = iv_xtestl    " Test mode
    WITH xausbel  = abap_true    " Output the documents cleared
    WITH xnausbel = abap_true    " Output the documents not cleared
    WITH x_fehler = abap_true    " Output the error messages
    EXPORTING LIST TO MEMORY     " To not generate a spool
    AND RETURN.

 

ABAP type for the collected data

In my final code, a variable of type TT_CLEARING_RESULT is exported by the enhancement to the ABAP memory, and imported after SUBMIT SAPF124, which is defined as follows:

    TYPES:
      BEGIN OF ts_doc_item_to_be_cleared,
        bukrs TYPE bseg-bukrs,
        belnr TYPE bseg-belnr,
        gjahr TYPE bseg-gjahr,
        buzei TYPE bseg-buzei,
      END OF ts_doc_item_to_be_cleared.
    TYPES tt_doc_item_to_be_cleared TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ts_doc_item_to_be_cleared WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    TYPES:
      BEGIN OF ts_clearing_doc_created,
        bukrs TYPE bseg-bukrs,
        belnr TYPE bseg-belnr,
        gjahr TYPE bseg-gjahr,
      END OF ty_ clearing_doc_created.
    "! Batch Input errors
    TYPES tt_journal_sapf124 TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bdcmsgcoll WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    TYPES:
      BEGIN OF ts_clearing_result
        s_clearing_doc_created   TYPE ts_clearing_doc_created
        t_doc_item_to_be_cleared TYPE tt_doc_item_to_be_cleared,
        t_journal                TYPE tt_journal_sapf124,
      END OF ts_clearing_result.
    TYPES tt_clearing_result TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ts_clearing_result WITH DEFAULT KEY.

Depending on the selection criteria when calling SAPF124, the result may contain many rows, some failing, others succeeding. For example, to clear 2 documents A 2024 and B 2025, which we know can be cleared, unfortunately the selection will be wide and there may be 4 documents selected: A 2024, A 2025, B 2024, B 2025.

In the result, there will likely be failures on A 2025 and B 2024, and a success for A 2024 and B 2025. Since we are only interested in the 2 documents A 2024 and B 2025, we must search the result for the row concerning them.

Example of collected data of type TT_CLEARING_RESULT :

Only the 6th line shows a success, the clearing document 0900002549 has been created to clear the documents shown in the component T_DOC_ITEM_TO_BE_CLEARED:

entry.title: Accelerate Your Role in SAP Supply Chain Transformation: Join Cloud Acceleration Partner Sessions
entry.content:
Today’s supply chains are evolving faster than ever—and SAP’s customers are moving with urgency to modernize both their manufacturing and planning landscapes. To help partners lead these critical transformations, SAP is offering two dedicated sessions that introduce the SAP Supply Chain Management Evolution Program, designed to equip you with the insight and confidence needed to guide customers through their next steps.

Why These Sessions Matter

Across manufacturing and planning, SAP customers are shifting from long‑established on‑premise solutions to the next generation of cloud‑based platforms:

  • SAP ME/MII → SAP Digital Manufacturing
  • SAP APO and ERP-based planning → SAP Integrated Business Planning (SAP IBP)

Both transformations represent major opportunities for partners. But they also require a clear understanding of the Customer Evolution Program—SAP’s structured approach to helping customers modernize with minimal risk and maximum long‑term value.

 What You’ll Gain by Attending

Each session is designed to give partners a practical understanding of:

  • How SAP is guiding customers through cloud transformation in manufacturing and planning
  • The purpose and structure of the SAP Supply Chain Management Evolution Program
  • The vital role partners play in helping customers adopt SAP Digital Manufacturing and SAP IBP
  • Real examples of customer transformation journeys and what drives success
  • Where partners can access resources, enablement, and next steps

By attending, you’ll leave with a clearer view of how to position the program with your customers—and how to identify opportunities to accelerate their transformation journey.

 Register for the Sessions

We encourage all partners supporting manufacturing and supply chain planning to join:

  • February 26 – SAP ME/MII → SAP Digital Manufacturing
  • March 4 – SAP APO/ERP → SAP Integrated Business Planning

These sessions are your opportunity to understand how SAP is helping customers evolve—and how you, as a partner, can play a central role in that journey.

entry.title: Re: Matching SAP S/4HANA Cloud user logins with SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) user logins for live data
entry.content: I would recommend bundling your SAC tenant with SAP Cloud Identity Services and then configure CIS to use the same corporate IDP as your S/4 system. After that is done, you should be able to log into SAC under that same user.

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance
entry.content:
Strengthening Access Visibility, Automation, and Governance

As organizations continue to modernize their system landscapes, access governance must adapt to increasing complexity. Managing users, roles, groups, and entitlements across hybrid and cloud environments calls for clearer visibility, smarter integration options, and dependable audit support.

The Q4 2025 updates to SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance (IAG) introduce a fresh set of enhancements spanning application integrations, APIs, job management, and reporting. These additions bring more flexibility, improved insight into access data, and expanded options for governing access. As a result, this helps teams work more efficiently as their environments grow and evolve.

Let’s take a closer look at what’s new in this release!

 

Seamless Application Integration with SAP HANA Cloud

SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance now supports deeper integration with SAP HANA Cloud, enabling synchronization of users, groups, and group authorizations. This integration lays the foundation for consistent access governance by supporting risk assessment, user provisioning with group assignments, and certification processes, all from a centralized governance layer.

Please refer to the following for more information: Help Portal Documentation

 

User Filter Support Across All Applications

User filter functionality is now extended to all SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance integration scenarios. This enhancement allows customers to exclude users who do not need to be governed, ensuring governance efforts remain focused and effective.

Please refer to the following for more information: Help Portal Documentation

 

Enhanced Access Request API for Greater Flexibility

The Access Request API has been enhanced by removing the mandatory domain field and introducing flexible user identification options. Users can now be retrieved using identifiers such as email, global user ID, or a universal search parameter.

Please refer to the following for more information: Help Portal Documentation

 

Manage Jobs - Change History Visibility

A new change history capability has been introduced in the Manage Jobs application. Administrators can now view updates made to jobs, including pause and resume actions, as well as the users responsible for those changes.

Please refer to the following for more information: Help Portal Documentation

 

Business Role Coverage Report

The new Business Role Coverage Report provides clear insights into how user roles are mapped within business roles. With smart filters, intuitive selection tools, and easy download options, administrators can quickly identify gaps and ensure proper role coverage.

Please refer to the following for more information: Help Portal Documentation

 

Unassociated Access Report

Embedded within the Business Coverage Report, the Unassociated Access Report helps administrators identify roles and groups that are not linked to any business roles. Users can drill down into details and refine searches using smart filters to pinpoint specific unassociated accesses.

Please refer to the following for more information: Help Portal Documentation

 

Access Report

The Access Report provides a comprehensive view of all users along with their assigned and unassigned accesses. It also shows how accesses relate to business roles, making it easier to validate access origins and appropriateness.

Please refer to the following for more information: Help Portal Documentation

 

Access Usage Report

The new Access Usage Report combines the functionality of the previously separate Unused Access and Actively Used Access reports into a single unified view. It supports on-premise and ABAP-based systems, including SAP HANA on-premise and SAP ERP.

Please refer to the following for more information: Help Portal Documentation

 

Closing Thoughts

These enhancements mark another strong step forward for SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance, bringing greater clarity, flexibility, and control to how organizations manage access across increasingly complex landscapes. From deeper integrations and APIs to unified reporting and stronger audit transparency, this release is designed to help teams govern access with confidence, efficiency, and precision.

As access governance continues to evolve alongside cloud and hybrid environments, SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance remains committed to delivering practical, scalable innovations that reduce risk, simplify operations, and support compliance at every stage. We’re excited to see how these capabilities help you strengthen your governance processes, and we look forward to continuing this journey with you as even more improvements arrive in upcoming releases.

For more details and configuration guidance, please refer to the SAP Help Portal documentation for SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance.

entry.title: Service broker error: Service broker apimanagement-apiportal-trial failed with: Forbidden
entry.content:
Integration Suite and API Management capability is enabled (Settings -> Runtime) on a BTP free trial.

I am able to test APIM Proxy no problem. But I can't subscribe or create a instance:

cf create-service apimanagement-apiportal-trial apiportal-apiaccess apim_apiportal_access_01 \
-c '{"role":"APIPortal.Administrator"}'

or

1. BTP Cockpit → Subaccount → Instances and Subscriptions → Create
2. Service: API Management, API portal
3. Plan: apiportal-apiaccess
4. Runtime Environment: Cloud Foundry
5. Space: dev
6. Instance Name: e.g. apim_apiportal_access_01
7. Next → Parameters
8. Paste JSON:

{"role":"APIPortal.Administrator"}

Creation Failed
Couldn't create an instance of service 'API Management, API portal' with plan 'apiportal-apiaccess'.

Service broker error: Service broker apimanagement-apiportal-trial failed with: Forbidden

entry.title: Re: Insert Token - performance
entry.content:
Hi @heidirobinson,
It was long time ago,but I think is not possible.

entry.title: Re: ABAP RAP - Action is not Updating the UI (unmanaged /...
entry.content:
@wjanes 

First, you need to update the entity by `MODIFY ENTITIES` statement.  After that, re-read the updated entity via `READ ENTITIES` and then pass the output to the result parameter.

Example form Help Portal:

  METHOD set_status_accepted.

    " Modify in local mode: BO-related updates that are not relevant for authorization checks
    MODIFY ENTITIES OF /DMO/I_Travel_M IN LOCAL MODE
           ENTITY travel
              UPDATE FIELDS ( overall_status )
                 WITH VALUE #( FOR key IN keys ( %tky      = key-%tky
                                                 overall_status = 'A' ) ). " Accepted

    " Read changed data for action result
    READ ENTITIES OF /DMO/I_Travel_M IN LOCAL MODE
      ENTITY travel
         ALL FIELDS WITH
         CORRESPONDING #( keys )
       RESULT DATA(travels).

    result = VALUE #( FOR travel IN travels ( %tky      = travel-%tky
                                              %param    = travel ) ).

  ENDMETHOD.

Is this possible to set up using processing rules?

entry.title: SAP Download Manager error (Unable to read basket content)
entry.content:
Hello support,

when trying to authenticate with SAP Download Manager, Basic and my S-User the attached error message is generated.

Already tried: New version of SAP Download Manager, another workstation, password reset.

Hint: I can see the contents of the download basket at: Also please see SAP case DIGITS/DIGITS - MFA deactivation didn't help.

Looking for your assistance. Thanks.

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP Download Manager error (Unable to read basket con...
entry.content:
Regarding to your issue, please kindly note that SAP Download Manager does not work with multi-factor authentication (MFA) or SAP Universal ID (UID).

If your S-user is linked to UID, do not enter the UID password in SAP Download Manager. The UID password is the one you use to logon SAP for Me.

Instead, enter your individual S-user ID and Password as mentioned in KBA 3016110. 

If you do not remember the password of your S-user, kindly use below link to reset it. When reset your password, please input the S-user ID instead of email address:

Forgot My Password

Then you will use your download manager normally as before.

You can still use your UID password to logon SAP for Me.

entry.title: Can you please help me to become an administrator for BTP platform
entry.content:
Hi Team

I am currently facing issue to enable joule on my realm as per email below from the support. Could you please assist on this matter as this issue has been going around the circle for quiet sometime.



Thank you for contacting SAP Support.

Initially, you are able to operate this on the SAP for Me platform, but after a detailed review, we discovered some issues.

We've identified the current issues:PERSON_NAME's account has full authorizations  at the installation level but lacks Edit User Data and Edit System Data authorizations.While Your account, SPHONE_NUMBER, lacks some authorizations at installation level DIGITS, preventing you from performing certain actions or submitting cases from system 

Since there are authorization gap between your S-User and PERSON_NAME's, we recommend you to report a case under XX-SER-FORME first, let support to help on this further. 

If you need any further assistance, please do not hesitate to contact us again. 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Can you please help me to become an administrator for...
entry.content:
And you are both super admin and cloud admin for your company account, which means you have the highest and most authorizations for your company.

If there are any other S-user is missing authorizations ,please refer to below KBAs to assign authorizations to them:

1511008 - How to add or change S-user ID authorizations - SAP for Me

1614304 - How to use the Copy Authorization feature for S-user IDs - SAP for Me

entry.title: IBP-KF Calculation at aggregated Qty to a Specific Product
entry.content:
Hello,

I need expert guidance on a key figure (KF) calculation requirement.

We have a mother pack that consists of multiple SKUs, and there is an attribute called Mother Pack Active (Y/N). The requirement is to aggregate the Actuals Quantity of all related SKUs and copy the aggregated value into a new key figure where Mother Pack Active = 'Y'.

(all products sum into one product based on Active status)

Please let us know the best approach to achieve this in IBP.

Regards,

BK

entry.title: Accout code which is posted by FAGL_FCV automatically
entry.content:
Hi all,

I would like to ask about Account code which is posted by FAGL_FCV automatically.

When I execute FAGL_FCV, SAP will automatically calculate valuation gain or loss and post using specific account code.

I want to change the specific account code which is automatically used by "Valuation area".

Do you know if it is possible?

entry.title: Re: CR for VS installation of SP 39 gives message "the op...
entry.content:
Hi Patrick,

What version of VS are you using?

Did you download

CR for Visual Studio SP39 64b installer (VS 2022 and above)

or 

CR for Visual Studio SP39 32b installer (VS 2019 and below)

When installing did you right click on the EXE and select Run As Administrator?

Look in: C:\Program Files (x86)\SAP BusinessObjects\InstallData\logs

And then the date stamped folder ans then open setupengine.log

Search for "error" or that message and see what it shows.

Try doing a Repair install and see if you get the same message.

Send me the log file if you can.

Don

entry.title: he user faces an issue with SAP for Me platform:

All the tenants are greyed out. We cannot provision them and its really impacting our timelines.

(Screenshot attached - issue.png)

Also, yesterday there was another issue with SAP4Me platform - provisioning section didn’t load for this S-User.


--- Support Assistant ---

Q: What issue do you have with Provisioning app?
A: Is 'Start Provisioning' button grey out and not clickable?

--- Issue Date (UTC) ---
DIGITS-12-31 16:28:13

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: he user faces an issue with SAP for Me platform:
entry.content:
According to KBA 3428706 - Start Provisioning button for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Production system is greyed out - SAP for Me , the successful provisioning of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Test system is a prerequisite before moving forward with the provisioning process for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Production.

Thus could you please adjust the entries as 50 as the screenshot then start provisioning for the test and Customizing environment at first.

In short, first trigger all the environments with 'start provisioning “available, then check if the gray buttons turn available, and continue triggering the other environments.

entry.title: Re: Will there be support for Crystal Reports Runtime 64...
entry.content:
Hello,

CR for VS follows the same time line as Crystal Reports Designer, so the end of life is typically a few years in the future.

CR products supports the current less 2 versions. And the dates are extended each time a new release is shipped.

CR for VS only has full versions available so always keep it up to date, typically 2 releases per year.

To many users, so don't expect CR or CR for VS ever to be discontinued, note CR for VS 32 bit is end of life now, only 64 bit is available in the new SP 40 release since there is no reason to support 32 bit applications anymore...

entry.title: new email address didn't receive UID verification code
entry.content:
Our new colleague wants to add her PERSON_NAME email address to her Univeral ID.
When she enters the email address no email with verification code is sent to this new email address.
How to go next?

Can anyone help here?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: new email address didn't receive UID verification code
entry.content:
Dear customer,

If user has not received an email with the verification code from noreply-uid@sap.com, could you pls kindly:

  • check and make sure if the entered email address is correct and valid. If not, enter the correct email address again
  • click 'Resend verification code' to request for a new verification code

This is documented in KBA 2948398 - Did not receive verification code email when setting up SAP Universal ID.

If still no any verification code sent to the email address, feel free to create case under component XX-SER-UID with the issue details.

Hope the above information helps.

Thank you and regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: How to re-generate license key for SAP BusinessObjects Business Intelligence platform
entry.content:
Hi team

We generate 200 license within one license key before, and there is no available material now.

can we revoke existing license key and generate new two license key with 100 number each ?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to re-generate license key for SAP BusinessObject...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

For BOBJ products, if the current license key quantity needs to be reduced, yes, kindly delete the existing license key, then the quantity 200 will be released back.

Then pls create a new license key with the proper number of quantity (e.g. 100) again.

This is documented in KBA 2446681 - Best practices of creating and updating SAP BusinessObjects license keys and system data - SAP for Me:
--------------------------------------------------------------------------

  • If it is required to reduce the quantity in an existing license key; delete the existing license key and then request a new license key for the correct quantity.
  • If it is required to increase the quantity in an existing license key, do one of the following:
    • Delete the existing license key and then request a new license key for the correct quantity
    • Request a license key for the additional quantity required as long as the total licenses per deployment complies with your license agreement

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

Hope this helps. 

Thanks and regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM - no supported use cases available for...
entry.content:
Whether this is a new SAP Cloud ALM tenant ? Usually, after the the cloud service tenant has been imported to the Landscape Management the supported use case should be listed.
In case the symptom persists, could please raise a case to SV-CLM-INF-LMS to help investigate this further.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA PUBLIC CLOUD EDITITION - CONTENT UPGRADE
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

According to the relevant SAP information, my understanding is that after four weeks of the software upgrade, SAP will release the new version of the content upgrade.

  1. Can I understand that the content upgrade contains business process content, meaning that it automatically generates transport requests (TRs) related to business processes, which the functional team will then take over? (Does the content upgrade only contain TRs related to business processes?)

  2. Does the content upgrade have a version? For the next release, is the version of the Content Upgrade 2602?

  3. When SAP upgrades the software version for each system, does it also generate any TRs?

  4. The Hotfix Collection also generates TRs. Does it also have a version, and does it need to be imported into Q and P systems?

  5. Both Hotfix TRs and Content Upgrade TRs are named “BC Upgrade.” How can we differentiate between them?

entry.title: Re: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Send PDF with XML file via email to your business partner
entry.content:
I am having an issue with the logo.

The pdf is sent without the logo. Is it related to the base64 convertion?

Thank you.

entry.title: Need Help Verifying Phone Number
entry.content:
I'm having trouble verifying the phone number for BTP Trial account.

I'm getting an error message: "We did not send you a code because you are not allowed to request phone calls. Please contact your system administrator." When I’m trying to request a code.

I'm currently living in Vietnam. Is there a way to verify via SMS or other way?

entry.title: Re: Translation Functionalities
entry.content:
Hi Melissa,

If your customer is using Enable Now Cloud, they can access the Translation Hub licenses using their Enable Now Cloud subscription.  This will make it much faster and easier to translate the content, rather than using the manual translation options that you discuss below.

If it is a Cloud subscription, please watch this video to understand how to use that capability.  If it is an on premise version of Enable Now, please confirm in this post and I will answer your questions, assuming that you do not have access to a Translation Hub license because a separate Translation Hub license is required for Enable Now on premise.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: How to Enable LMS in SAP SuccessFactors for the first...
entry.content:
Hi @Bilal2 I suggest reviewing the SAP Community Guide and KBAs for SuccessFactors Learning Integration with BizX/Platform.

  • Integrate BizX to LMS – Quick Steps of Implementation 
  • 3079390 - Best Practices Before BizX + LMS Integration 
  • 3209088 - Integrating SuccessFactors BizX and Learning Post 1H 2022 Release

entry.title: How to hide fields /Tabs in SAP PPM?
entry.content:
1.- Right-click and then click on Technical Help.

 

2.- Click on the Component Configuration link

 

3.- Select the RIH_EDIT variant and choose CREATE CUSTOMIZING in the "Other Functions" drop-down menu.

 

4.- In Customizing Mode, you can hide elements. Select the views or subviews you want to hide and check in "Hidden Element" checkbox. E.g. Select the View "Deliverables", check in the "Hidden Element" and finally "Save" the changes.

 

5.- If you access the item you will see that the "Deliveries" tab does not appear.

You can create one customizing transport request and accomodate all the changes in a single TR

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA PUBLIC CLOUD EDITITION - CONTENT UPGRADE
entry.content:
Hi Thao,

Below are some of my observations/opinions in my experience in form of answers (with links)to your questions.

Yes—your understanding is aligned with SAP’s described timeline:   software upgrade and content upgrade are decoupled, and the   content upgrade follows after the software upgrade.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/53e36b5493804bcdb3f6f14de8b487dd/076da842605b4105b6adfbf325695941.html

Q1)Content Upgrade  is essentially SAP-delivered   business process / business configuration content changes .

However, I would  not  phrase it as  it contains  only  business process TRs because in practice, you should treat these as  SAP-delivered content/configuration transports , and the functional team typically validates business impact while Basis/IT manages the transport steps

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/faq-on-upgrading-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition/ba-p/13657921

 Q2) Yes,SAP’s 2602 upgrade preparation blog states clearly:
The 2602 Content becomes available after the D-System is upgraded, and a new transport is created … to deliver the 2602 Content to the T-System  . 

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/get-ready-for-2602-upgrade-of-sap-cloud-erp-public-edition/ba-p/14307594

So, for the 2602 wave, it is correct to expect a corresponding  2602 Content delivery via transports. 

Q3) This depends heavily on what you mean by generate TRs and which deployment model you are in.
-   the TR creation that SAP explicitly calls out is tied to the CBC Business Content Upgrade   (content transports created in Dev and moved to T/P).

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/faq-on-upgrading-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition/ba-p/13657921

In   classic  ABAP upgrade (e.g., SUM on on-prem/private cloud)
It’s normal to have SPDD/SPAU adjustment processes where transport requests are created/used to store adjustment results (different world than public cloud CBC). https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-and-automation-discussions/how-to-handle-spdd-and-spau-when-starting-the-upgrade-with-a-non-dev-system/m-p/11933199

Q4) 

In my exp., BC Upgrade transport can contain hotfix content and is automatically deployed to your systems, and if it wasn’t released automatically, you may need to take ownership and release it.

you should ensure the hotfix-related content is consistently promoted per your landscape rules, but the exact mechanics depend on whether SAP auto-schedules vs. you manually release/import in your setup.

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/how-to-check-the-content-included-in-the-quot-bc-upgrade-quot-transport-in/qaq-p/13855884

Q5)Remember,
- If it appears in the bi-weekly patch cycle  , it is likely hotfix-related (HFC).

- If it appears specifically after   D-system major upgrade completion   and is used to deliver 2602 Content to T-system, it aligns with release content upgrade transports or say BC Upgrade !

Hope it helps!

If I have answered your queries, please help in marking post-answered.

Thanks & Cheers!

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors Onboarding: Improving Form I-9 Document Upload Controls: What Changed & What’

entry.title: Re: Mass change of Cost Center and Profit center validity...
entry.content:
Yes — in SAP S/4 HANA fiori, there are fiori applications that allow you to update validity dates for Cost centres and Profit centers in a single step / mass change without having to update each master individually.

Mass Change – Cost centres

This app allows you to change attributes (including validity dates) of multiple cost centres in one run.

Mass Change – Profit centres (or within Manage Profit Centers depending on your S/4HANA release)

You can adjust the validity dates / other attributes for multiple profit centres

These mass change apps help you close the existing cost and profit centres as of 31.12.2025 by updating the “Validity To” date, and then you can create new ones effective 01.01.2026.

✔ Use the mass change fiori apps to set Validity To = 31.12.2025

✔ Use Migration Cockpit to upload the new cost centres and profit centreswith Validity From = 01.01.2026

 

Regards 

Edalada saiteja

entry.title: Re: Joule Studio: How to Create Document Grounding
entry.content:
Hi @Dan_Wroblewski,

Thanks for the very detailed documentation.

I have a question regarding Document Grounding refresh behavior in Joule Studio and how it interacts with Resource Groups and AI Launchpad.

  1. When a Resource Group is created via Joule Studio and initially points to an object store with a limited set of documents, what happens if we later add more documents to the same object store?

    • Will the existing Resource Group automatically pick up the new documents, or is a manual refresh / reprocessing required?

  2. Additionally, if we manually add a Data Pipeline in AI Launchpad to an existing Resource Group that was originally created through Joule Studio, is this a supported and recommended approach?

    • Will this work seamlessly, or are there any limitations or conflicts we should be aware of?

Looking forward to your guidance on the recommended way to handle document updates and refresh scenarios.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Make field read-only in table but editable in Object Page
entry.content:
I have a custom app where in the Object Page, there is section for a table where i can add new business partner.

But I want the fields to be read-only when it is viewed as a table in the Object Page.

I want it to be editable when I access the row into another the BP Object Page.

I already tried using dynamic feature control, but the logic cannot tell whether the app is in table view or in object page view. Need your help on this matter. Thanks!

entry.title: Inspection lot for Inspection document 17* does not exist (Message Class: QPLEXT)
entry.content:
Hi Community,

I'm configuring Quality Management in Decentral EWM, where I would like to give UD in QA32.

Once the GR is done in EWM, Inspection document in getting generated in EWM. But, Queue is getting stuck in ERP with Inspection lot for Inspection document 17* does not exist (Message Class: QPLEXT) this error.

I have verified the configs, but still not able to resolve it. I'm attaching the screenshot below of the Queue and its error.

I would really appreciate the help

entry.title: SAP BW Production Data Refresh
entry.content:
Hi,

We have a requirement to refresh the SAP BW data from the Production system into the SAP BW QA system. Our goal is to refresh only the data, ensuring that system settings and configurations in the QA system are not overwritten by those from the Production environment.

Could you please advise on the recommended approach or procedure to perform this activity?

Thank you.

BR


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP BW Production Data Refresh
entry.content:
Dear customer,

Thank you for contacting SAP Support.

Regarding to your concern, would you please refer to the question '?23. Are the configuration files also saved during a backup?' in Note 1642148 - FAQ: SAP HANA Database Backup & Recovery ?  
By default, the customer specific configurations which have been modified from the default value are not included in HANA database backup.

======================================================
23. Are the configuration files also saved during a backup?

By default the customer-specific configuration settings (.ini files that have been changed from the default) are not backed up as part of a data backup.

Starting with HANA 2 SPS06 you can Backup and Recovery Customer-Specific Configuration Settings.

Backing Up Customer-Specific Configuration Settings

BACKUP DATA Statement

RECOVER DATA Statement

RECOVER DATABASE Statement

Configuration files that contain customer-specific changes can be also backed up and restored manually.

Configuration files that contain customer-specific changes are located by default in the following directories:

For global configuration settings:

?$DIR_INSTANCE/.. /SYS/global/hdb/custom/config? also alias ?cdcoc can be used

For host-specific configuration settings:

?$SAP_RETRIEVAL_PATH?

Configuration files are only created in these directories if customer-specific changes are made to them after installation. If no customer-specific changes have been made, these directories may be empty.
======================================================

Also, you can refer to 'Backing Up Customer-Specific Configuration Settings' in HANA Admin guide. 
If you still have concern, you may copy the configuration files(*.ini) and store them in a separate directory as backup.

Best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: IBP-KF Calculation at aggregated Qty to a Specific Pr...
entry.content:
Here's the suggestion - In copy operator, apply filter to source key figure Actuals Qty as MP Active = Y. Model target key figure as Actuals Qty - MP. Aggregation level should be at Mother Pack in copy operator.


Regards,
Piyush
